description:
Implement the standard 27-item Kirby Monetary Choice Questionnaire (MCQ) in PsychoPy Builder to measure delay discounting (impulsivity).

Create a conditions file at /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/kirby_mcq.csv with columns named 'sir' (Small Immediate Reward), 'ldr' (Large Delayed Reward), and 'days' (Delay in days) using the following data:

Small Magnitude: (19, 25, 53), (14, 25, 19), (10, 25, 12), (11, 30, 7), (20, 55, 7), (15, 35, 13), (25, 60, 14), (24, 35, 29), (34, 50, 155)
Medium Magnitude: (54, 55, 117), (54, 60, 111), (54, 80, 20), (27, 50, 21), (49, 60, 89), (40, 55, 62), (47, 50, 160), (41, 75, 20), (33, 80, 14)
Large Magnitude: (31, 85, 7), (22, 85, 20), (67, 75, 119), (34, 35, 186), (25, 30, 80), (69, 85, 91), (78, 80, 162), (54, 75, 117), (80, 85, 157)

Develop the experiment at /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/delay_discounting.psyexp. The task must present the 27 trials in a randomized loop named 'trials'. Each trial should display the immediate reward ("Today: $[sir]") and the delayed reward ("In [days] days: $[ldr]") side-by-side, accepting 'q' for the left option and 'p' for the right option. Do not calculate k-values.
